title: When the guard fails: 0 rows = someone else won
prompt_md:
The flip side of a guarded update is the **no-op**. Try to set the `rating` of the movie with `id` `1` (Dune) to `8.0`, but only if its current rating is below `8.0`. Return the `title`.

Dune is already `8.5`, so the guard fails — **no rows match**, and you get back an empty result. In a real backend, getting **0 rows affected** is your signal that someone else already won the race; you'd show 'already taken' instead of erroring.
starter_code: UPDATE movies SET rating = 8.0 WHERE id = 1 AND ... RETURNING title;
hints: Dune is 8.5, which is NOT < 8.0, so zero rows match and nothing is returned.
reference_solution: UPDATE movies SET rating = 8.0 WHERE id = 1 AND rating < 8.0 RETURNING title;
